articles.title: GameStop’s eBay Gambit: A $46 Billion Threat to Pokémon Collectors and Retro Gamers
articles.summary: GameStop is reportedly preparing a bid to acquire eBay, a move that could give it near-total control over the online secondhand market for trading cards and retro games. With eBay valued at $46 billion and GameStop at $12 billion, the debt from such a merger could cripple both companies and devastate collector communities.

articles.title: Solateria Lights the Way: How a Soulslike Embraced Difficulty Options Without Losing Its Edge
articles.summary: Solateria, a hand-drawn Metroidvania from Studio Doodal, breaks the Soulslike mold by offering flexible difficulty settings and helpful map markers. Its parry-based combat and puzzle-like boss fights remain demanding, but the game proves that accessibility and challenge can coexist beautifully.

articles.title: Before Awakening, Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War Paved the Way 30 Years Ago
articles.summary: Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War, released in 1996 for the Super Famicom, introduced the generation-spanning narrative that later defined Awakening. Its massive maps, intricate politics, and dramatic rescue missions set a storytelling standard that would ultimately save the series from collapse.
